contentBlocks.original:
श्री गणेशाय नमः ।
श्री महादेव उवाच ।
ततो रामः स्वयं प्राह हनुमन्तमुपस्थितम् ।
श‍ऋणु यत्वं प्रवक्ष्यामि ह्यात्मानात्मपरात्मनाम् ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī gaṇēśāya namaḥ ।
śrī mahādēva uvācha ।
tatō rāmaḥ svayaṃ prāha hanumantamupasthitam ।
śa‍ṛṇu yatvaṃ pravakṣyāmi hyātmānātmaparātmanām ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sri Ganeshaya Namah. Sri Mahadeva (Shiva) speaks: Then Rama himself addressed the approaching Hanuman — 'Listen, I shall now impart to you the secret teaching...'

contentBlocks.original:
आकाशस्य यथा भेदस्त्रिविधो दृश्यते महान् ।
जलाशये महाकाशस्तदवच्छिन्न एव हि ।
प्रतिबिम्बाख्यमपरं दृश्यते त्रिविधं नभः ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ākāśasya yathā bhēdastrividhō dṛśyatē mahān ।
jalāśayē mahākāśastadavachChinna ēva hi ।
pratibimbākhyamaparaṃ dṛśyatē trividhaṃ nabhaḥ ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.translation: Just as the sky is perceived in three apparent divisions — the great unbroken sky (mahakasha), the sky as reflected in a body of water, and the sky seemingly enclosed within a container — so too is pure consciousness (chit) of three kinds.

contentBlocks.original:
बुद्ध्यवच्छिन्नचैतन्यमेकं पूर्णमथापरम् ।
आभासस्त्वपरं बिम्बभूतमेवं त्रिधा चितिः ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
buddhyavachChinnachaitanyamēkaṃ pūrṇamathāparam ।
ābhāsastvaparaṃ bimbabhūtamēvaṃ tridhā chitiḥ ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.translation: Consciousness as limited by the intellect is one form; the full unlimited consciousness is another; and the reflected-consciousness (abhasa) is the third, as the original (bimba). Thus awareness is of three kinds.

contentBlocks.original:
साभासबुद्धेः कर्तृत्वमविच्छिन्नेऽविकारिणि ।
साक्षिण्यारोप्यते भ्रान्त्या जीवत्वं च तथाऽबुधैः ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sābhāsabuddhēḥ kartṛtvamavichChinnē'vikāriṇi ।
sākṣiṇyārōpyatē bhrāntyā jīvatvaṃ cha tathā'budhaiḥ ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.translation: The sense of doership (kartritva) is superimposed by error on the witness-consciousness, which is in reality unchanging and unlimited. Similarly, the state of being an individual soul (jivatva) is superimposed by the ignorant.

contentBlocks.original:
आभासस्तु मृषाबुद्धिरविद्याकार्यमुच्यते ।
अविच्छिन्नं तु तद्ब्रह्म विच्छेदस्तु विकल्पितः ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ābhāsastu mṛṣābuddhiravidyākāryamuchyatē ।
avichChinnaṃ tu tadbrahma vichChēdastu vikalpitaḥ ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.translation: The reflected-consciousness (abhasa) is called a false product of avidya (ignorance); the undivided Brahman is called unlimited. Any apparent division from Brahman is only imagined.

contentBlocks.original:
अविच्छिन्नस्य पूर्णेन एकत्वं प्रतिपद्यते ।
तत्त्वमस्यादिवाक्यैश्च साभासस्याहमस्तथा ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avichChinnasya pūrṇēna ēkatvaṃ pratipadyatē ।
tattvamasyādivākyaiścha sābhāsasyāhamastathā ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.translation: The undivided jiva gains identity with the full Brahman through the Mahavakyas such as 'Tat tvam asi' (That thou art). And through 'aham asmi' (I am), the reflected-consciousness also realizes that identity.

contentBlocks.original:
ऐक्यज्ञानं यदोत्पन्नं महावाक्येन चात्मनोः ।
तदाऽविद्या स्वकार्यैश्च नश्यत्येव न संशयः ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aikyajñānaṃ yadōtpannaṃ mahāvākyēna chātmanōḥ ।
tadā'vidyā svakāryaiścha naśyatyēva na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.translation: When knowledge of the unity between the individual self and Brahman arises through the Mahavakya, then avidya together with all its products is destroyed without doubt.

contentBlocks.original:
एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भक्तो मद्भावायोपपद्यते
मद्भक्तिविमुखानां हि शास्त्रगर्तेषु मुह्यताम् ।
न ज्ञानं न च मोक्षः स्यात्तेषां जन्मशतैरपि ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētadvijñāya madbhaktō madbhāvāyōpapadyatē
madbhaktivimukhānāṃ hi śāstragartēṣu muhyatām ।
na jñānaṃ na cha mōkṣaḥ syāttēṣāṃ janmaśatairapi ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.translation: The devotee who truly knows this attains oneness with Me. For those averse to My devotion, wandering lost in the thickets of mere scripture — there is neither true knowledge nor liberation.

contentBlocks.original:
इदं रहस्यं हृदयं ममात्मनो मयैव साक्षात्कथितं तवानघ ।
मद्भक्तिहीनाय शठाय न त्वया दातव्यमैन्द्रादपि राज्यतोऽधिकम् ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ rahasyaṃ hṛdayaṃ mamātmanō mayaiva sākṣātkathitaṃ tavānagha ।
madbhaktihīnāya śaṭhāya na tvayā dātavyamaindrādapi rājyatō'dhikam ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.translation: This is My secret Heart, proclaimed directly to you by Me Myself, O sinless one. This must not be given by you to one devoid of devotion to Me, nor to a deceitful person — not even under pressure from Indra or any other.
Thus ends the Sri Rama Hrudayam from the Balakanda of the Adhyatma Ramayana. (Colophon)
